description_long: This is a large, multi-engine airplane with a streamlined fuselage and a classic straight-wing design. The wings are mounted at a mid-level on the fuselage and feature four visible propeller engines, each housed in a nacelle. The airplane is predominantly gray with bold red accents along the fuselage, wings, and tail, giving it a striking appearance. The tail section consists of a single vertical stabilizer and a horizontal stabilizer, both colored red. The cockpit is located at the front of the fuselage and appears to be enclosed, with a slightly raised canopy. The overall design suggests a vintage or historical aircraft, likely used for transport or bomber roles.
description: A large, gray and red multi-engine airplane with straight mid-mounted wings, four propeller engines, and a single vertical tail. The cockpit is enclosed and positioned at the front of the fuselage.
description_view_0: Front-left view showing the four propeller engines, the enclosed cockpit, and the red accents on the wings and tail.
description_view_1: Rear-left view highlighting the red tail section, horizontal stabilizer, and the full span of the straight wings.
description_view_2: Rear-right view focusing on the tail assembly and the alignment of the engines along the wings.
description_view_3: Front-right view displaying the propeller engines, cockpit, and the red and gray color scheme.
attribute_wingspan_configuration: Mid-wing configuration with straight wings, no visible winglets, and no pronounced dihedral or anhedral angles.
attribute_engine_type: Four piston engines mounted in nacelles along the leading edge of the wings.
attribute_tail_design: Conventional tail with a single vertical stabilizer and horizontal stabilizers.
attribute_landing_gear_type: Not clearly visible, but likely retractable based on typical design; no fixed gear or floats apparent.
attribute_cockpit_placement: Forward-mounted, enclosed cockpit with a slightly raised canopy at the front of the fuselage.
